perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” means all oral and written information furnished by, or on behalf of, PHCS to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below), which is not ge nerally known to the public, including, without limitation, all trade secrets, products, procedures, manuals, guidelines, reports, communic ations, information regarding names and requirements of current and prospective suppliers, vendors and customers, tec hnical information regarding current and prospective products and programs and the development and/or acquisition of future products, programs a nd enhancements, discoveries, concepts and ideas, nature and results of research and development activities, tec hnical information on product or program performance and reliability, processes, formulae, techniques, “know -how,” specifications, any “protected health information” as defined in HIPAA regulations, 45 C.F.R. Parts 160 and 164, as amended from time to time , and any other material or information related to the business or activities of PHCS, whether or not protectible by patent, copyright or other laws, whe ther furnished before or after the date of this Agreement, together with any additional information der ived from such information and contained in all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or records prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives, as well as al l copies and other reproductions thereof, whether in writing or stored or maintaine d in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” means all oral and written information furnished by, or on behalf of, PHCS to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below), which is not ge nerally known to the public, excluding any information that is readily discoverable through public sources or reverse engineering, but including, without limitation, all trade secrets, products, procedures, manuals, guidelines, reports, communic ations, information regarding names and requirements of current and prospective suppliers, vendors and customers, tec hnical information regarding current and prospective products and programs and the development and/or acquisition of future products, programs a nd enhancements, discoveries, concepts and ideas, nature and results of research and development activities, tec hnical information on product or program performance and reliability, processes, formulae, techniques, “know -how,” specifications, any “protected health information” as defined in HIPAA regulations, 45 C.F.R. Parts 160 and 164, as amended from time to time , and any other material or information related to the business or activities of PHCS, whether or not protectible by patent, copyright or other laws, whe ther furnished before or after the date of this Agreement, together with any additional information der ived from such information and contained in all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or records prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives, as well as al l copies and other reproductions thereof, whether in writing or stored or maintaine d in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'excluding any information that is readily discoverable through public sources or reverse engineering,' we create an in-text contradiction with the later requirement in Section 7 for the return or destruction of 'all Confidential Information,' regardless of form. This means that the recipient could argue that information obtained through reverse engineering, though related to PHCS's confidential processes, does not need to be returned or destroyed, creating uncertainty in enforcement of the confidentiality clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Destruction/Return of Confiden tial Information . Upon PHCS’ request, all Confidential Information (and all copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof), whether in writing or stored or maintained in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices, sh all be returned or destroyed (such destruction to be certified in writing) by an authorized officer and not retained in any form or for any reason.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exclusion to the definition of 'Confidential Information,' stating that information readily discoverable through public sources or reverse engineering is not considered confidential. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which mandates the return or destruction of 'all Confidential Information' without exception. The changed text creates a loophole where the recipient could argue that certain information, even if related to PHCS, is exempt from the return/destruction requirement, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Exclusions from Confidential Information . Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in Parag raph 1, Confidential Information does not include information which (i) was or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives; or (ii) was or becomes available to the Recipient on a non -confidential basis from a source other than PHCS, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS or otherwise prohibited from transmitti ng the information to the Recipient; or (iii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished by or on behalf of PHCS, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS in respect thereof or otherwise pr ohibited from transmitt ing the information to the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Exclusions from Confidential Information . Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in Parag raph 1, Confidential Information does not include information which (i) was or becomes generally available to the public other than as a direct result of a deliberate disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives with the express intention of making it public; or (ii) was or becomes available to the Recipient on a non -confidential basis from a source other than PHCS, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS or otherwise prohibited from transmitti ng the information to the Recipient; or (iii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished by or on behalf of PHCS, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS in respect thereof or otherwise pr ohibited from transmitt ing the information to the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By adding '...as a direct result of a deliberate disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives with the express intention of making it public', the exclusion now requires proof of intent. This conflicts with the indemnification clause in Section 9. Now, if information becomes public due to negligence rather than deliberate action, it's unclear whether the indemnity applies, introducing ambiguity. This change makes it harder for PHCS to claim indemnification if the breach wasn't intentional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Indemnification . The Recipient agrees to indemnify and hold harmless PHCS, and its aff iliates, and its and their shareholders, directors, officers, members, managers, partners, employees and agents (collectively, the “Indemnified Parties”) from and against any an d all costs and expenses (including without limitation reasonable attorneys’ fe es) incurred by or on behalf of any Indemnified Party arising out of any breach of any provision of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text excludes information that becomes publicly available from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The changed text adds the condition that the information becoming public must not be 'a direct result of a deliberate disclosure by the Recipient...with the express intention of making it public'. This significantly narrows the exclusion. The 'Indemnification' clause states that the Recipient will indemnify PHCS for 'any breach of any provision of this Agreement'. The change creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity as to whether PHCS can claim indemnification if the information was disclosed negligently rather than deliberately, as intent is now a factor. This contradicts the original all-encompassing indemnification clause, which seemingly doesn't distinguish between intentional and unintentional breaches.

perturbation.original_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS, unless such use is required by law. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the phrase 'unless such use is required by law' creates a contradiction with Section 4, which restricts disclosure of the existence of the agreement itself. If a legal request demands revealing the existence of a confidentiality agreement with PHCS, it is unclear whether this 'required by law' clause overrides the explicit non-disclosure of the agreement's existence, thereby introducing ambiguity in compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition, without the prior written consent of PHCS, except to the extent Confidential Information may be disclosed to Representatives of the Recipien t or pursuant to the exception identified in Paragraph 7, neither the Recipient nor its Representatives will disclose to any person (which shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporat ion company, group, partnership, trust, association or individual) (i) that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, (ii) that they have inspected any portion thereof, (iii) the existence of, or any of the terms, conditions or other fa cts with respect to, this Agreement or its business relationship with PHCS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the phrase 'unless such use is required by law' to the Use of Confidential Information clause. The contradicted text explicitly prohibits disclosing the existence of the agreement or its terms. The 'required by law' exception creates a direct contradiction because it's unclear whether a legal mandate to disclose the agreement's existence overrides the explicit non-disclosure obligation. This introduces ambiguity and a potential conflict, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall only be responsible for grossly negligent or willful misconduct regarding the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the wording in section 3 from 'The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use...' to 'The Recipient shall only be responsible for grossly negligent or willful misconduct...', introduces an in-text contradiction with the indemnification clause in Section 9. The indemnification clause holds the recipient responsible for 'any breach' while section 3 limits it to gross negligence/willful misconduct. It becomes unclear if the recipient needs to indemnify PHCS for all breaches or only for breaches related to gross negligence or willful misconduct by its representatives. This change significantly limits the Recipient's liability compared to Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Indemnification . The Recipient agrees to indemnify and hold harmless PHCS, and its aff iliates, and its and their shareholders, directors, officers, members, managers, partners, employees and agents (collectively, the “Indemnified Parties”) from and against any an d all costs and expenses (including without limitation reasonable attorneys’ fe es) incurred by or on behalf of any Indemnified Party arising out of any breach of any provision of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives", while the changed text modifies this to "The Recipient shall only be responsible for grossly negligent or willful misconduct regarding the Confidential Information by its Representatives". This directly contradicts the indemnification clause in Section 9, which holds the Recipient responsible for "any breach" of the agreement. The changed text significantly narrows the scope of the Recipient's responsibility, creating a clear contradiction between the liability described in Section 3 and the indemnification obligation in Section 9.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary set forth herein, in the event that the Recipient or its Representatives are requested or become legally compelled (by oral questions, interrogatories, request for information or documents, subpoena , civil investigative demand or similar pr ocess) to disclose any of the Confidential Information or take any other action prohibited hereby, the Recipient shall provide PHCS with prompt written notice so that PHCS may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not sought or obtained or that the compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is waived, the Recipient agrees that it will furnish on ly that portion of the Confidential Information which, in the reasonable opinion of its counsel, the Recipient is compelled to disclose without stan ding liable for contempt or suffering other censure or penalty. The Recipient further agrees that it will exercise its best effor ts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to that portion of the Confidential Information which subject to the compelled disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary set forth herein, in the event that the Recipient or its Representatives are requested or become legally compelled (by oral questions, interrogatories, request for information or documents, subpoena , civil investigative demand or similar pr ocess) to disclose any of the Confidential Information or take any other action prohibited hereby, the Recipient shall provide PHCS with prompt written notice, only if reasonably possible, so that PHCS may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not sought or obtained or that the compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is waived, the Recipient agrees that it will furnish on ly that portion of the Confidential Information which, in the reasonable opinion of its counsel, the Recipient is compelled to disclose without stan ding liable for contempt or suffering other censure or penalty. The Recipient further agrees that it will exercise its best effor ts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to that portion of the Confidential Information which subject to the compelled disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'only if reasonably possible' to the notification requirement in Section 8, this creates an in-text contradiction. The original text mandates 'prompt written notice.' The added condition now contradicts the original mandate. This introduces uncertainty and could be interpreted as a failure to provide notification. This contradiction affects section 10, which outlines the injunctive relief because a delayed notification might cause irreparable harm to PHCS.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief . The parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agre ement, PHCS might be irreparably harmed and unable to be made whole by monetary damages. It is accordingly agreed that PHCS, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or equity, will be entitled to seek an injunction to remedy bre aches of this Agreement and/or to compel specific performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text adds "only if reasonably possible" to the requirement for providing prompt written notice. This contradicts the original requirement of 'prompt written notice' because it introduces a condition where notice might not be prompt or even given. The contradicted text (Injunctive Relief) states that PHCS might be irreparably harmed in the event of any breach of the agreement. Delaying or omitting the notification due to the 'only if reasonably possible' clause directly undermines PHCS's ability to seek timely injunctive relief, creating a direct contradiction and rendering the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient’s obligations of confidentiality and nondisclosure shall survive termination of PHCS’ business dealings with the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient’s obligations of confidentiality and nondisclosure shall survive termination of PHCS’ business dealings with the Recipient for a period of two years.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'for a period of two years,' to Section 11, regarding the term, creates an in-text contradiction with the overarching premise of the entire agreement. While Section 11 now has a limited term, sections that imply indefinite confidentiality, such as 7 (destruction/return of confidential information) and 10 (injunctive relief), create a legal inconsistency. If a breach occurs after the two-year term, it's unclear whether PHCS can still seek injunctive relief, because Section 10 doesn't explicitly limit the time within which it can be invoked, while Section 11 defines that confidentiality only survies termination for two years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief . The parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agre ement, PHCS might be irreparably harmed and unable to be made whole by monetary damages. It is accordingly agreed that PHCS, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or equity, will be entitled to seek an injunction to remedy bre aches of this Agreement and/or to compel specific performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a time limit (two years) on the confidentiality obligations, whereas the contradicted text regarding injunctive relief implies that such relief is available indefinitely. This creates a direct conflict, because the contradicted section doesn't explicitly limit the time within which it can be invoked, while the changed text defines that confidentiality only survives termination for two years. If a breach occurs after the two-year term, it's unclear whether PHCS can still seek injunctive relief. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
